drafts.title: Why Enterprise AI Agents Need a Control Plane
drafts.hook: Most agent demos look impressive. The hard question is whether the agent should be allowed to act inside an enterprise workflow.
outline: The gap between prototype agents and production agents
Why governance cannot be an afterthought
Control plane as the missing layer
What must be governed: agents, tools, models, data, approvals, audit
Open-source reference implementation as a learning and adoption path
drafts.call_to_action: I am publishing an open-source AgentOps control-plane reference project and would value feedback from enterprise architects and AI product leaders.

drafts.title: Why Agent Governance Should Be Deterministic First
drafts.hook: An LLM can explain a decision, but it should not be the only mechanism enforcing a high-risk enterprise control.
outline: LLM reasoning vs auditable control decisions
Blocked actions as valuable learning signals
How to combine LLM assistance with inspectable enforcement
drafts.call_to_action: I am collecting real-world AgentOps governance patterns. Suggestions and critiques are welcome.

drafts.title: The Real Enterprise Agent Challenge: Data, Tools, and Accountability
drafts.hook: The risky part of agentic AI is not only generation. It is context access, tool use, side effects, and accountability.
outline: Audit event bus and decision history
Why production readiness requires evidence, not optimism
drafts.call_to_action: I would like to compare this control-plane approach with how other teams are preparing agent programs.
